text.title: Team Development Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete rules for charter authoring, skills matrix use, retrospective quality, and agent safety constraints.

rule.statement: Co-write the team charter with the team in a 60-minute workshop; never let an agent author it solo without team input.
rule.rationale: Agent-authored charters are generic and lack the buy-in that only participation creates.

rule.statement: Every retrospective action item must have a single named owner and a sprint-deadline; reject "the team will look into this".
rule.rationale: Actions without owners are not actions; they are intentions that expire at the end of the retro.

rule.statement: Refresh skills matrix quarterly; treat it as planning input, not performance data.
rule.rationale: Skills matrices used for performance review create gaming; used for planning they create learning opportunities.

rule.statement: Retro outputs must not name individuals; preserve psychological safety in all summarised outputs.
rule.rationale: Individual naming in retro summaries chills honest participation and can cause interpersonal harm.

rule.statement: Retro themes must be backed by evidence from at least two distinct sprints before being called a pattern.
rule.rationale: Single-sprint observations are noise; patterns require recurrence to be actionable.

rule.statement: Evaluate training and pairing options before proposing a hire to close a skills gap.
rule.rationale: Agents default to "hire" because it is the clearest action; training and pairing are faster, cheaper, and improve retention.

antipattern.description: Recommending "more standups" or "better communication" as the fix for most retro themes.
antipattern.reason: This is an LLM default; it rarely addresses the root cause and wastes ceremony time on the wrong intervention.

antipattern.description: Setting skills matrix scores to 3 (median) when the agent has no information about a person.
antipattern.reason: Unknown should be "unknown", not a guess; phantom scores drive incorrect gap-planning decisions.

antipattern.description: Flagging recurring themes as "new" each sprint because the agent has no session memory.
antipattern.reason: Pass last N synthesised theme files into context; without history the agent cannot detect patterns.
